subject: 📊 Daily Portfolio: +0.64% | AVGO near stop after earnings reset + VRDN PDUFA June 30
html_body:
Daily Portfolio Check

Tuesday, June 10, 2026 — Kuwait Time (GMT+3)

Total P&L (Open Positions vs Cost)

+$3.95 (+0.64%)

Today (est.)

+$0.45 (+0.07%)

🎯 Withdrawal goal: $832.71 / $1,000 — $167.29 to go (83.3% there)

AMD exit locked +$5.11 net yesterday. Now 4 positions: AVGO near stop after historic earnings selloff; NVDA within 2% of stop; TSM approaching TP1; LLY steady at all-time high after TP1 trim.

⚠️ Decisions needed

1. AVGO: Check with your broker — June 9 intraday touched $370.33, which is BELOW your $379 stop. Verify whether the stop triggered. If still holding, price is $387.73, 2.25% above stop. Do NOT add.
2. NVDA: $208 current, stop at $204 — only 1.9% gap. If it breaks $204 on volume, exit. Watch daily close closely.
3. ACHV PDUFA June 20: 10 days away. If you want a catalyst play on this one, decision needed by Friday June 13.
4. TSM TP1 $435: Don’t trim here — trim math shows only +$0.14 net after $3 commission. Hold or raise TP1 to ~$448.

Position Snapshot

Status Ticker Price* Day P&L $ P&L %
⚠ WATCH NVDA $208.00 ~flat −$13.04 −4.35%
✓ HOLD LLY $1,155.00 +2.08% +$15.79 +16.09%
⚠ WATCH AVGO $387.73 −1.75% −$7.66 −5.90%
⚠ TP1 NEAR TSM $428.00 +0.28% +$8.88 +10.21%
TOTAL — +0.07% +$3.95 +0.64%

*Intraday estimates — not confirmed 4 PM ET closes. Verify on Yahoo Finance after 4:30 PM ET.
Sources: NVDA $208 intraday (Yahoo Finance / CNBC Jun 10); LLY $1,155 intraday, prev close $1,131.42 confirmed (Yahoo Finance); AVGO $387.73 confirmed Jun 10 session (Yahoo Finance); TSM ~$428 est. (StockAnalysis / Benzinga Jun 10). Account total: $832.71 (positions $618.90 + cash $213.81). Portfolio last updated Jun 9.

Position Detail

NVDA ⚠ WATCH $208.00 (−4.35% vs entry)

Post-May-20-earnings drift has pushed NVDA 4.4% below the $217.40 entry. Stop at $204 is 1.9% below current price — you are inside the 2% watch zone. Key June 9 event: Intraday dipped to $199.34 (50-day SMA zone) then bounced back — that support held and the close was above stop. AI capex thesis unchanged (hyperscaler combined spend ~$725B in 2026, +77% YoY). Stop at $204 is technically valid — it sits just above the pre-breakout base. However: consider whether your hard stop is at $204 or your mental close-based stop. If hard stop, check your broker for any June 9 fill.

Stop: $204 ⚠ 1.9% away TP1: $240 TP2: $260 TP3: $285

TP validation: TP1 $240 = above recent ATH ~$211; no prior structural resistance at that level. Re-derive once price recovers above entry. TP2 $260 / TP3 $285 are longer-term targets contingent on H2 FY27 earnings cycle.

LLY ✓ HOLD $1,155.00 (+16.1% | ATH territory)

TP1 executed June 8: Trimmed 0.0421 shares at $1,152.76 — Gross P&L +$6.66, Commission −$3.00, Net: +$3.66. Holding 0.0986 shares. Today: +2.08% to ~$1,155, driven by orforglipron (Foundayo) Phase 3 weight-loss data and retatrutide sleep apnea / T2D results. LLY hit all-time high $1,182.73 on June 8. Thesis strengthening — GLP-1 moat widened materially. Next target TP2 $1,243 is 7.6% above current. TP2 validation: Above current ATH ($1,182); sellside consensus $1,200–$1,400, so $1,243 is within the consensus band. Valid target.

Stop: $915 TP1: $1,114 ✓ done TP2: $1,243 → TP3: $1,393
AVGO ⚠ WATCH POST-EARNINGS RESET $387.73 (−5.9% vs entry)

Q2 FY2026 earnings (June 3): Beat EPS ($2.44 vs $2.40) but AI chip revenue guidance for next quarter came in at $16B vs $17.2B buyside whisper — triggered a historic −12.6% drop, erasing ~$280B market cap (one of the largest single-day megacap selloffs in history). Stock has been recovering from $370 lows but remains volatile.

⚠ CRITICAL: June 9 intraday low was $370.33 — that is BELOW your $379 stop. If you have a hard stop order at $379, it may have been triggered June 9. Check your broker immediately. If still holding: $387.73 is 2.25% above stop. Do NOT average down (HIGH risk score). Let the stop do its job. If it closes below $379, exit.

Stop validation: $379 is a real level — it marks the post-earnings base and the June 3–4 settlement zone. Median 1-month recovery after comparable AVGO selloffs: +8%. Thesis weakened (AI revenue growth decelerating signal) but not broken (AI revenue still +100%+ YoY absolute). Hyperscaler capex spending intact.

Stop: $379 ⚠ 2.3% away TP1: $474 TP2: $536

TP1 $474 = logical pre-earnings recovery target (stock was $480–520 before June 3 drop). TP2 $536 = prior ATH zone. Both require AI revenue growth to resume in Q3 guide.

TSM ⚠ WATCH → TP1 $428.00 (+10.2% vs entry)

Quietly becoming the 9th most valuable company globally (~$2.2T market cap). TP1 at $435 is 1.6% away. Commission math on trim at $435: sell 30% = 0.0672 shares → Gross P&L +$3.14 | Commission −$3.00 | Net: +$0.14. Commission eats the entire profit. Recommendation: Do NOT trim at $435. Raise TP1 to $448 (near 52-week high $450.16) where the trim is meaningful. At $448: 0.0672 shares → Gross +$4.01 | Net +$1.01. Still thin, but real. Full thesis intact — AI foundry monopoly, no geopolitical escalation signals.

Stop: $357 TP1: $435 (1.6% away — HOLD) TP2: $485

Suggested: Raise TP1 from $435 to $448 (near 52-week ATH $450.16). Trim there or hold all to TP2 $485 for the real exit.

📡 Catalyst Plays

June 10, 2026 — FDA Radar

15 searches run. 12 candidates identified, 3 survive pre-screen. All halal statuses ⚠ unverified — check Musaffa before any entry. Prices are estimates; run “analyze [TICKER]” for the full deep dive.

VRDN Viridian Therapeutics ⚡ WATCH 3/5

PDUFA: June 30, 2026 — 20 days | veligrotug BLA for Thyroid Eye Disease (TED) | Priority Review + BTD

Mkt cap ~$600M est.  |  Clinical stage  |  Appeared in 4/15 searches (highest signal)

Approval probability

~72% — IGF-1R mechanism validated in TED; Priority Review base rate ~83%; slight discount vs. established Tepezza (teprotumumab) competition. Two Phase 3 trials confirmed positive.

✓ Approval: ~+80–100% — approaches analyst avg target $33–34 (prior high zone)
💀 CRL: ~−55–65% — typical Priority Review rejection; floor near $5–7 cash/book

EV on $150 position (after $3 buy + $3 sell commissions):
72% × (+$126) + 28% × (−$90) = +$90.72 − $25.20 = +$65.52 expected

Smart money: Hosting Goldman Sachs Healthcare Conference — 24 analysts cover with avg target ~$34 vs ~$17 current est. = 100% implied upside.
Halal: ⚠ Unverified — clinical-stage, minimal revenue; verify cash holdings on Musaffa before entry.

Say “analyze VRDN” for the full 8-section deep dive.

VERA Vera Therapeutics ⚡ WATCH 3/5

PDUFA: July 7, 2026 — 27 days | atacicept for IgA nephropathy | Phase 3 ORIGIN met primary endpoint early

Mkt cap ~$1.5B est.  |  Lowest binary risk in this group — Phase 3 already succeeded

Approval probability

~83% — Phase 3 ORIGIN met primary endpoint early; FDA review is largely confirmatory. IgA nephropathy base rate for Phase 3 successes in nephrology ~85%.

✓ Approval: ~+40–60% — limited surprise upside (Phase 3 success already known)
💀 CRL: ~−40–50% — low probability; Phase 3 success provides a floor

EV on $150 position:
83% × (+$72) + 17% × (−$67.50) = +$59.76 − $11.48 = +$48.28 expected

Smart money: Appeared in 3 search sources; FDA already aligned with company on early ORIGIN endpoint data.
Halal: ⚠ Unverified — clinical-stage biotech. Check Musaffa.

Say “analyze VERA” for the full 8-section deep dive.

ACHV Achieve Life Sciences ⚡ WATCH 3/5 — URGENT

PDUFA: June 20, 2026 — 10 days | Cytisinicline for smoking cessation | IF entering, decide by Friday June 13

Mkt cap ~$150M est. (very small)  |  Natural plant-derived compound (cytisine)  |  High binary volatility

63% approval probability — crowded smoking cessation space; FDA has specific labeling requirements; positive Phase 3 but not slam-dunk. Bull: +100–150% on approval. Bear: −65–75% on CRL.

WATCH not INVESTIGATE: pre-score 3/5 + halal unverified. Halal: ⚠ Unverified — natural compound, likely halal; verify on Musaffa urgently if considering entry.
Decision window closes Friday June 13 for a June 20 PDUFA.

Say “analyze ACHV” for the full 8-section deep dive — time-sensitive.

💵 Cash Deployment — $213.81 available

Cash $213.81 | Reserve $6 | Buy commission $3 | Deployable: $204.81 net capital. No existing position is a good add (NVDA near stop, AVGO HIGH risk, TSM approaching commission-eating TP1, LLY at ATH). New catalyst play is the best use.

Option A — VRDN (PDUFA June 30, 20 days) — Recommended

Buy ~$150 in VRDN at market (~10–12 shares est. at ~$12–15). Event: June 30 PDUFA, Priority Review, TED. Upside +80–100%. Downside −55%. EV: +$65.52. Position = 18% of portfolio (within 30% cap $249.81). Remaining cash after: $213.81 − $150 − $3 = $60.81 (above $6 reserve ✓).
Blocker: Verify halal on Musaffa FIRST.

Option B — VERA (PDUFA July 7, 27 days)

Buy ~$150 in VERA at market. Phase 3 already met primary endpoint — lower binary risk. Upside +40–60%. EV: +$48.28. Better odds, less upside. Good alternative if VRDN halal check fails.

Recommended: Option A (VRDN). Higher EV, earlier PDUFA, stronger source signal (4 vs 3 searches). Both require Musaffa halal check before entry. Your call.

🔄 Rotation Suggestions

No offensive rotations from existing positions today.

Three reasons: (1) NVDA and AVGO are both within 2.5% of their stops — anti-rotation rule applies; never sell into a level you’re already defending. (2) TSM trim at TP1 clears only $0.14 net after commission — not worth the trade. (3) LLY is performing — TP1 done, let TP2 run.

The right action is deploying idle cash (Option A above) into a new name rather than cannibalizing existing positions. AVGO is the highest-risk name, but that rotation happens via the stop naturally if $379 breaks — not by selling at a loss now at $387.

🔍 Intelligence Layer

Top Screener Candidates

VRDN — PDUFA June 30, TED indication, Priority Review. Highest multi-source confidence (4/15 searches). Best upside EV today. Needs halal check.
VERA — PDUFA July 7, IgA nephropathy. Phase 3 already succeeded = lowest binary risk. Best risk-adjusted play if you want smoother odds or VRDN fails halal.
ACHV — PDUFA June 20 (10 days). Most urgent. Tiny market cap + unverified halal = WATCH. Decide by Friday.

Risk Scores

NVDA | 35/100 | MODERATE | Near stop $204 (1.9%); 42% concentration; below entry
LLY | 30/100 | LOW | Thesis intact; ATH momentum; TP1 executed cleanly
AVGO | 58/100 | HIGH | Post-earnings guide miss; June 9 stop breach; AI decel signal
TSM | 38/100 | MODERATE | Geopolitical tail risk; approaching TP1; healthy trend

What to do — Wednesday June 11

1. AVGO: Call your broker — did the June 9 intraday dip to $370 trigger your $379 stop? If still holding, watch $379 daily close. Exit if it closes below.
2. NVDA: Watch $204 support. If it breaks on volume (not a wick), exit. If it holds and bounces above $212, consider raising stop to $201 (below June 9 wick $199).
3. TSM: Raise TP1 in your notes from $435 to $448. Don’t trim at $435 — commission eats the profit.
4. VRDN / VERA: Check Musaffa.com for halal status of both tickers. If VRDN confirmed halal, Option A is live before June 30 PDUFA. Decision by Friday.
5. ACHV: If interested, this is a Friday decision or it’s too late for reasonable pre-PDUFA positioning.

📚 Today you learned

Earnings Whisper Numbers — Why AVGO Dropped on a Beat

AVGO reported June 3 and hit every public number: EPS $2.44 beat, AI revenue +100% YoY, guidance $29.4B ahead of sellside $28.53B. Stock fell 12.6%. This isn’t irrational — it’s the whisper gap. Sellside publishes a consensus everyone can see. Buyside funds run their own models and price in a different number — the whisper. Before AVGO’s print, buyside was positioned for Hock Tan to raise the AI semiconductor FY27 run-rate above $120B. He held at $100B+. Funds that had positioned for a bigger raise sold. The headline said “beat” but the real question was: did they beat the expected magnitude of the beat? They didn’t. This exact mechanic is why NVDA can beat official EPS by 20% and still sell off. For every earnings you hold through, find the whisper beforehand (EarningsWhispers.com, FinTwit pre-earnings consensus). If you can’t find it, assume the whisper is 10–15% above consensus for high-momentum AI names.

Also today: IV crush — implied volatility collapses post-earnings, destroying option value even when the stock moves in your direction; why buying options into earnings is a hard trade even when you’re directionally right.

Research output, not financial advice. Past performance does not guarantee future results. Verify halal compliance quarterly on Zoya or Musaffa. All catalyst names (VRDN, VERA, ACHV) halal status unverified — check Musaffa before entry.

Prices as of June 10, 2026 (intraday estimates, not confirmed 4 PM ET closes): NVDA ~$208 (Yahoo Finance/CNBC intraday; Jun 8 close $208.64 confirmed) | LLY ~$1,155 (Yahoo Finance intraday; prev close $1,131.42 confirmed) | AVGO $387.73 (Yahoo Finance Jun 10 session confirmed) | TSM ~$428 est. (StockAnalysis/Benzinga Jun 10). Broadcom earnings: CNBC/Motley Fool Jun 3 2026. AVGO historic selloff: Yahoo Finance Jun 4 2026. LLY ATH: Motley Fool Jun 8 2026. TSM milestone: Benzinga Jun 10 2026. VRDN PDUFA: FDA Tracker/BioPharmaWatch/CatalystAlert. VERA ORIGIN: Stock Titan/BioPharma Dive.

Halal: Musaffa AAOIFI, verified May 11, 2026 for NVDA, LLY, AVGO, TSM — re-verify quarterly. Generated June 10, 2026, Kuwait time (GMT+3). Portfolio last updated: 2026-06-09.
